quiz_title: Mapei Ceramic Line — Beginner Quiz (Very Simple)
audience: Sales / showroom staff — entry level / new joiners
source: MAPEI_PRODUCT_KNOWLEDGE.xlsx — built from official Mapei TDSs
difficulty: all very_easy — single-fact recall, one obvious data point per question
design_notes: Distractors are still real values from other Mapei products in the same column, but the correct answers are the most basic, most-asked facts — bag size, colour, what it is, etc.

questions.question: What is the standard bag size of Keraflex Maxi S1 Zero?
questions.explanation: Keraflex Maxi S1 Zero comes in a 25 kg bag — the standard cementitious adhesive bag size.

questions.question: What is the bag size of Mapeset?
questions.explanation: Mapeset is supplied in a 25 kg bag.

questions.question: What colours does Ultralite S2 come in?
questions.explanation: Ultralite S2 is available in both white and grey — like most Mapei cementitious adhesives.

questions.question: What colours does Adesilex P10 come in?
questions.explanation: Adesilex P10 is white only — the white powder is designed to enhance the colour of glass mosaics.

questions.question: Mapesil AC is which type of product?
questions.explanation: Mapesil AC is a one-component silicone sealant supplied in a cartridge.

questions.question: Mapesil LM is which type of product?
questions.explanation: Mapesil LM is a one-component neutral silicone sealant.

questions.question: Keracolor FF is which type of product?
questions.explanation: Keracolor FF is a cementitious grout — it fills the joints between tiles.

questions.question: Granirapid is famous for being…
questions.explanation: Granirapid is Mapei's fast-setting two-component adhesive — that's why it carries the 'F' in its C2F S1 classification.

questions.question: Which letter in a Mapei adhesive code means 'cementitious'?
questions.explanation: C = Cementitious adhesive. R = Reaction resin (Keralastic). D = Dispersion. G = Grout.

questions.question: Which letter in a Mapei adhesive code means 'reaction resin' (e.g. Keralastic)?
questions.explanation: R = Reaction resin adhesive — used for Keralastic and Keralastic T (two-component polyurethanes).

questions.question: What does the number '2' mean in a code like C2TE?
questions.explanation: 1 = normal performance, 2 = improved performance per EN 12004.

questions.question: What does the letter 'T' mean in a code like C2TE?
questions.explanation: T = slip-resistant — tiles don't slide down a wall while the adhesive is wet.

questions.question: What does the letter 'E' mean in a code like C2TE?
questions.explanation: E = extended open time (>30 minutes). Adhesives without E typically have only ~20 min open time.

questions.question: How many components does Granirapid have?
questions.explanation: Granirapid is two-component — Part A powder + Part B latex.

questions.question: How many components does Keralastic have?
questions.explanation: Keralastic is a two-component polyurethane: Component A (resin) + Component B (hardener).

questions.question: How many components does Mapesil AC have?
questions.explanation: Mapesil AC is one-component — comes ready-to-use in a cartridge, cures with humidity in the air.

questions.question: What is the cartridge size of Mapesil AC?
questions.explanation: Mapesil AC and Mapesil LM both come in 310 ml cartridges. Mapesil Z Plus is the smaller 280 ml.

questions.question: What is the cartridge size of Mapesil Z Plus?
questions.explanation: Mapesil Z Plus comes in a 280 ml cartridge — slightly smaller than AC and LM (both 310 ml).

questions.question: Which Mapei product is known as the 'lightweight' adhesive line?
questions.explanation: The Ultralite range (S1, S2, S2 Quick) are Mapei's lightweight adhesives — 15 kg bags with the same coverage as a 25 kg conventional bag.

questions.question: Kerapoxy is what kind of chemistry?
questions.explanation: Kerapoxy is a two-component epoxy resin — used as both grout and adhesive.

questions.question: Kerabond Plus is a one-component _____ adhesive.
questions.explanation: Kerabond Plus is a one-component cementitious adhesive (C2E classification).

questions.question: What is the standard storage / shelf-life of most Mapei cementitious adhesives in this range?
questions.explanation: Cementitious adhesives store 12 months. Reaction-resin products (Keralastic, Kerapoxy) and Mapesil AC store 24 months.

questions.question: Which standard sets the rules for cementitious tile adhesive classification (C1, C2, etc.)?
questions.explanation: EN 12004 / ISO 13007 is the European/international standard for tile adhesive classification. EN 15651 is for sealants. ASTM C1248 is the non-staining test for silicones.

questions.question: What does 'EC1 Plus' on a Mapei product label refer to?
questions.explanation: EMICODE EC1 Plus = very low emission of volatile organic compounds — the highest indoor air-quality rating. Almost all Mapei products in this range carry it.

questions.question: What is the minimum application temperature for most Mapei cementitious adhesives?
questions.explanation: Most cementitious adhesives apply at +5 °C minimum. Keralastic needs +10 °C minimum, Kerapoxy needs +12 °C minimum.

questions.question: Granirapid is mixed by pouring the powder into…
questions.explanation: Granirapid uses no water — pour the powder (Part A) into the latex (Part B) and mix mechanically.

questions.question: After mixing a cementitious adhesive, what should you do BEFORE applying it?
options: Let it rest for ~5 minutes, then re-mix
questions.correct_answer: Let it rest for ~5 minutes, then re-mix
questions.explanation: Standard Mapei procedure: mix → rest 5 min → re-mix. The rest period lets the polymers fully activate.

questions.question: Keracolor FF is available in how many colours from the MAPEI range?
questions.explanation: Keracolor FF is available in 16 colours from the MAPEI range. Mapesil AC has the largest palette at 40 + transparent.

questions.question: Which Mapei product in this range is described as a 'CO2-fully-offset' adhesive?
questions.explanation: The 'Zero' in Keraflex Maxi S1 Zero refers to its CO2-fully-offset status — Mapei's eco-conscious version of Keraflex Maxi.

questions.question: Which symbol in the workbook means a use is EXPLICITLY FORBIDDEN by the TDS?
questions.explanation: ✓ = supported, ✗ = explicitly forbidden, '—' = not mentioned (use cautiously). Standard Mapei TDS-matrix convention.
